forecasts.reasoning: The forecast date is April 2026, meaning the resolution date of June 1, 2025, has already passed. Historical evidence from mid-2025 confirms that while Ramzan Kadyrov publicly asked to resign in early May 2025, Russian President Vladimir Putin denied his request (Al Jazeera, May 19, 2025). Furthermore, reports from July 2025 (e.g., Meduza and Novaya Gazeta Europe) confirm that Kadyrov remained in office well past the June 1, 2025 deadline, with Meduza explicitly noting that despite rumors and his May meeting with Putin, 'nothing changing' regarding his position. Thus, he did not cease to be the Head of the Chechen Republic before June 1, 2025. I am outputting the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.

The NFL Competition Committee chose not to propose a ban on the 'Tush Push' at the March 2024 Annual League Meeting, leaving the play fully legal for the 2024 season. Given that peak controversy surrounding the play occurred during the 2023 season, the failure to secure 24 owner votes for a ban indicates a lack of sufficient support to outlaw it. Furthermore, with the retirement of Eagles center Jason Kelce, the play may be less successful and therefore less controversial in the upcoming season. Reports of a '1-yard behind' rule clarification largely reflect a past memo rather than a new restriction, as it merely enforces existing alignment rules. Because the urgency to ban the play has significantly receded, it is relatively unlikely (though still remotely possible due to aesthetic/injury concerns from Commissioner Goodell and some owners) that a ban or major restriction will be passed at the Spring 2024 or March/May 2025 meetings. Therefore, the probability is roughly 20%.
